internal_issues.description: Stale terminology: 'toroidal sump node' not found in V51.2 dome. Must cite V51.0 source or pivot to 'Sub-terrestrial structure'.
Quantitative error: H(17,000) = 1,188 km not 1,400 km. Fix: use r≈15,500–20,015 km range or correct H value.
Vague gravitational-index coupling claim with no mechanism or numbers.
Generic post-hoc boilerplate in detail_verdict_text — needs gravity-specific source citations (GRACE/GRACE-FO, Pappa 2019, Ferraccioli 2011).
Internal inconsistency: paragraph 2 uses geographic south pole (~55,000 nT), paragraph 3 uses South Magnetic Pole (~65,000–67,000 nT). Must align to one framing.
Overclaim: 'explicitly INTERMAGNET/CHAOS-7' — soften to sources the dome highlights.
Disjunction misses metaphysical retreat (aetheric claim abandons Maxwellian EM). Add third horn.
finding field says 'Dome adds no equation' — imprecise, dome does publish FSF formula. Use 'no dome-derived equation'.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome's own firmament math forbids 7.83 Hz at every radius — we now prove it
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: WIN-038 now leads with the pole-floor impossibility: the dome's published H(r) formula gives a minimum of 8.78 Hz at the pole (its most favorable point), rising to ~90 Hz at the rim. 7.83 Hz cannot be produced anywhere on the disc by any averaging method. The old '22 Hz average' framing was correct but weaker; the new argument is geometrically airtight.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Upgrades from 'miss by 3x' to 'impossible by geometry' — significantly stronger argument.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: P-wave shadow zone: dome's fixed wall can't produce rings around every earthquake
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: WIN-064 now documents the dome's verbatim 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall' claim (verified live on wins.html), then proves that a fixed geographic boundary cannot produce the observer-centered 104°-140° ring around every epicenter worldwide. Also exposes that the dome's live monitoring panel measures focal depth in km against a prediction about angular distance — comparing the wrong units with no comparison logic, yet marking pass:true unconditionally.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Two distinct new attacks in one WIN: geometric impossibility of fixed wall + live-panel unfalsifiability exposed.
